req.title: System-Admin updates benefit
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a benefit through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a system-admin can update a benefit via UI form, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and correct HTTP responses. I derive: (1) a positive EP/STC case covering the happy path from 'existing benefit' to 'updated benefit' with audit; (2) a NEG/DT case covering missing mandatory fields triggering a 400; (3) a BVA case targeting the name field at its maximum allowed length boundary; (4) a NEG case for unauthenticated access returning 401; (5) a NEG/DT case for forbidden role returning 403. Each test uses fixtures for auth/page setup and is idempotent via teardown helpers.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_benefit_happy_path
actions: Update the benefit name to a valid string
expected: Audit record is written for the update action
API returns 200 or 201 with entity id
trace: R-WEB-034: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_updates_benefit_happy_path(admin_page, benefit_factory, audit_client, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="Original Benefit")
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='edit-btn']").click()
    name_input = page.locator("[data-testid='benefit-name-input']")
    name_input.fill("Updated Benefit Name")
    page.locator("[data-testid='submit-btn']").click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    updated = page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='benefit-name']")
    expect(updated).to_have_text("Updated Benefit Name")
    audit_entry = audit_client.get_latest(entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit.id)
    assert audit_entry is not None
    assert audit_entry["action"] == "UPDATE"
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)

tests.name: test_admin_update_benefit_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
expected: UI displays validation error message on name field
Form is not submitted (benefit not updated)
API returns 400 if form bypass is attempted
trace: R-WEB-034: error pathway 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_benefit_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(admin_page, benefit_factory, api_client, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="To Validate")
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='edit-btn']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='benefit-name-input']").fill("")
    page.locator("[data-testid='submit-btn']").click()
    error_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-name']")
    expect(error_msg).to_be_visible()
    resp = api_client.put(f"/benefits/{benefit.id}", json={"name": ""})
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    still_original = api_client.get(f"/benefits/{benefit.id}").json()
    assert still_original["name"] == "To Validate"
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)

tests.name: test_admin_update_benefit_name_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: Max allowed name length is 255 characters
actions: Enter a benefit name exactly 255 characters long
expected: Benefit name stored equals exactly 255-character string
trace: R-WEB-034: mandatory field boundary at max length
R-WEB-034: positive update with boundary input
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_admin_update_benefit_name_at_max_boundary_length(admin_page, benefit_factory, api_client, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="Boundary Test")
    max_name = "B" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='edit-btn']").click()
    name_input = page.locator("[data-testid='benefit-name-input']")
    name_input.fill(max_name)
    assert len(name_input.input_value()) == MAX_NAME_LEN
    page.locator("[data-testid='submit-btn']").click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    stored = api_client.get(f"/benefits/{benefit.id}").json()
    assert stored["name"] == max_name
    assert len(stored["name"]) == MAX_NAME_LEN
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_benefit
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no session cookie)
actions: Attempt to navigate to /benefits in browser with no session
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_benefit(unauthenticated_page, anon_api_client, benefit_factory, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="Auth Test Benefit")
    resp = anon_api_client.put(
        f"/benefits/{benefit.id}",
        json={"name": "Should Fail"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    page = unauthenticated_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    expect(page).to_have_url(f"{app_url}/login")
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_updating_benefit
actions: Send PUT /benefits/<BENEFIT_ID> with viewer auth token
Navigate to /benefits as viewer and attempt edit
expected: UI does not show Edit button or shows access-denied message
trace: R-WEB-034: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_updating_benefit(viewer_page, viewer_api_client, benefit_factory, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="RBAC Test Benefit")
    resp = viewer_api_client.put(
        f"/benefits/{benefit.id}",
        json={"name": "Forbidden Update"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    page = viewer_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    edit_btn = page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='edit-btn']")
    denied_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='access-denied']") 
    assert edit_btn.count() == 0 or denied_msg.is_visible()
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)

tests.name: test_benefit_state_transitions_draft_to_active_on_update
preconditions: A benefit with id <BENEFIT_ID> exists in DRAFT state
expected: Benefit status transitions from DRAFT to ACTIVE
UI shows ACTIVE badge on the benefit row
API returns 200 or 201 with updated entity
trace: R-WEB-034: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_benefit_state_transitions_draft_to_active_on_update(admin_page, benefit_factory, api_client, audit_client, app_url):
    benefit = benefit_factory.create(name="State Test", status="DRAFT")
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_url}/benefits")
    page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='edit-btn']").click()
    status_select = page.locator("[data-testid='benefit-status-select']")
    status_select.select_option("ACTIVE")
    page.locator("[data-testid='submit-btn']").click()
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    active_badge = page.locator(f"[data-testid='benefit-row-{benefit.id}'] [data-testid='status-badge']")
    expect(active_badge).to_have_text("ACTIVE")
    updated = api_client.get(f"/benefits/{benefit.id}").json()
    assert updated["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    audit = audit_client.get_latest(entity="benefit", entity_id=benefit.id)
    assert audit["old_value"]["status"] == "DRAFT"
    assert audit["new_value"]["status"] == "ACTIVE"
    benefit_factory.delete(benefit.id)
